name: Tom Fitton
subject: Merry Christmas from Judicial Watch
body: Merry Christmas! [INSIDE JW] MERRY CHRISTMAS! As I wrote in the new Judicial Watch book “A Republic Under Assault [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21585&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaGJvb2suY29tL10s4oCdP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5 Christmas 2020 finds the foundations of our republic under attack. So, at a time like this, it may be best to step back and try to focus our hearts and minds on things that unite us. As we do, I am reminded of one of my favorite stories from Christmas past. On Christmas Day, 1776, the American Revolution appeared to be dead. George Washington’s Continental Army had been driven out of New Jersey. The British and Hessian troops, who assumed the serious fighting was over, had entered winter quarters. King George III and the British Parliament appeared set to continue an abusive set of policies that American colonists said deprived them of their rights as Englishmen. The battle cry of “No Taxation without Representation [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21586&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudS1zLWhpc3RvcnkuY29tL3BhZ2VzL2g2NDAuaHRtbF3igJ0/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDEyMjQwMDAyMDk= went unsatisfied. That all changed after Washington’s crossing of the Delaware River in the midst of a driving blizzard on December 24, 1776. The “Ten Crucial Days [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21587&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9yZXZvbHV0aW9uYXJ5bmoub3JnL2d1aWRlZF90b3VyL3Rlbi1jcnVjaWFsLWRheXMtNC9d4oCdP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5 that began with an audacious strike against the unsuspecting Hessian garrison in Trenton, culminating with the Battle of Princeton [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21588&F=T&drurl=aHR0cDovL3d3dy5oaXN0b3J5LmNvbS90aGlzLWRheS1pbi1oaXN0b3J5L3RoZS1iYXR0bGUtb2YtcHJpbmNldG9uP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5], reinvigorated the Revolution. Every year on Christmas Day, thousands of visitors and local residents would gather along the Delaware River to witness the reenactment [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21589&F=T&drurl=aHR0cDovL3d3dy51c2F0b2RheS5jb20vcGljdHVyZS1nYWxsZXJ5L25ld3MvMjAxNC8xMi8yNS8xNzc2LWRlbGF3YXJlLXJpdmVyLWNyb3NzaW5nLzIwOTAzODk3Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTIyNDAwMDIwOQ==] of Washington’s Crossing [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21590&F=T&drurl=aHR0cDovL3d3dy53YXNoaW5ndG9uY3Jvc3NpbmdwYXJrLm9yZy9wYXJrL2Nyb3NzaW5nLXJlZW5hY3RtZW50Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTIyNDAwMDIwOQ==]. As much as we all rightly celebrate The Spirit of 1776, in too many respects our nation is harmed by the progressive counter-revolution that undermines the republican form of government established by our Founding Fathers. Just before it left for Christmas, Congress authorized over a trillion dollars [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=21591&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGVmZW5zZW5ld3MuY29tL2NvbmdyZXNzL2J1ZGdldC8yMDIwLzEyLzIyL21hc3NpdmUtc3BlbmRpbmctYmlsbC1wYXNzZWQtYnktY29uZ3Jlc3Mtc2VudC10by10cnVtcC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnMmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9MjAyMDEyMjQwMDAyMDk=] in spending with little public debate or disclosure. That’s why our mission at Judicial Watch assumes a heightened importance going into 2021. If the ideals of our American Revolution seem like ghosts now in our “modern” government, it is worth recalling that the situation looked even more dire before the two Battles of Trenton and the Battle of Princeton. Yes, there were two Battles of Trenton. And they serve as a dual lesson of the audacity and perseverance we will now need in order to continue overcoming the odds we face. After crossing the Delaware River on Christmas night in 1776, Washington’s Continental Army surprised the Hessians who were staying in what is now the Old Barracks Museum and in other homes throughout Trenton. He re-crossed again four days later to confront the full brunt of the British Army led by General Charles Cornwallis. And the American rag-tag forces’ twin victories in those battles spurred our nation on to victory, and ought to give each of us cause for encouragement in light of contemporary challenges arguably far less serious than those our Revolution faced in Southern New Jersey 244 years ago. Washington himself saw the hand of Providence at work in his Christmas crossing. An abiding faith can go a long way, even in the darkest moments. There is one Christmas message that should give us indomitable courage for these troubling times. It is from Isaiah 9:6, and it defines the hope that lies within us all: “For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given – _and the government shall be upon his shoulders_.” Ultimately, man’s power is limited on this Earth, contrary to what today’s progressives and their antecedents might try to tell you. And we know that, in the words of one of my favorite Christmas carols, “the wrong shall fail, the right prevail.” Merry Christmas to you and yours from all of us here at Judicial Watch, and a happy New Year. Until Next Week... [Contribute] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=12711&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS05ODYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5] [32x32x1] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=13&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZmFjZWJvb2suY29tL0p1ZGljaWFsV2F0Y2gvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5] [32x32x2] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=14&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly90d2l0dGVyLmNvbS9qdWRpY2lhbHdhdGNoP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5] [32x32x3] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=15&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vdXNlci9KdWRpY2lhbFdhdGNoP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJzJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PTIwMjAxMjI0MDAwMjA5] [32x32x3] [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52400&L=1285&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaW5zdGFncmFtLmNvbS9qdWRpY2lhbHdhdGNoLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIwMTIyNDAwMDIwOQ==] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - body:
No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-z/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Finally a Deal, and Merry Christmas](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-j/)
With only two days to go until Christmas, you're busy. So was Congress, passing a $900 billion COVID relief package. You can read about it in this week's blog. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [The Georgia Runoff](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-d/)
The upcoming Georgia Senate runoffs are perhaps the most important races in recent memory. They will decide what kind of country we are for years to come. I talk about why that is in this week's blog. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Let's Not Repeat Obama's Mistake in Iraq](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-u/)
A further reduction of US troops in Iraq was recently announced. Is this a wise move? I think not, and here's why. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [Slaughter in Nigeria](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-n/)
It's happened again. The Islamist terror group Boko Haram has once again attacked innocent civilians in Nigeria. With the current COVID surge, the aftermath of the recent election and the upcoming holiday season, odds are you didn't see mention of this latest atrocity in most news coverage. In this week's blog, I discuss why this recent attack, or any terrorist threat, must not be ignored. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-p/) [Donate](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-x/) [Get a Yard Sign](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-c/) | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-q/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-a/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-juhdzuk-kujkulhluu-f/)

name: Mary Mancini
subject: 'Twas the Night Before the Inauguration
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/%E2%80%9CIt%20is%20Christmas%20in%20the%20heart%20that%20puts%20Christmas%20in%20the%20air.%E2%80%9D.png]
Dear Peter,

While we all wait impatiently for 2021 to get here - especially January 20, 2021 - we want to take the time to wish you a very happy and healthy holiday season (and send you this little bit of holiday cheer). We also want to thank you for your dedication and hard work during this very challenging year. Because of you, we are a stronger Tennessee Democratic Party.

All the best to you and yours in the New Year,
Mary, Kris, Emily, Evan, Julio, Maria, and Sandra

‘Twas the Night Before the Inauguration
(with apologies to Clement Clarke Moore)

'Twas the night before Inauguration, and all through the White House,
All the creatures were stirring, even the orange-haired louse.
The stockings were nailed to the chimney with fanfare,
As if to say, "Try and take us down, try-hard, if you dare."
Trump children were hiding, snug under rugs by their beds,
While visions of staying forever danced in their heads;
And Melania in her 'kerchief, and Trump, a MAGA hat,
Had just barricaded in, still hoping for a coup d’etat,
When out on the north lawn there arose such a clatter,
They sprang from the bed to see what was the matter.
Away to the window they flew like a flash,
Tore open the shutters and threw up the sash.
The moon on the crest of the new-fallen snow
Gave the luster of daylight to the rose garden below,
When, what to their plundering eyes should appear,
But a Corvette Stingray, revving up in high gear,
With a dashing bold driver, in blue head to toe,
They knew in a moment it must be Uncle Joe.
More rapid than eagles Joe’s cabinet came,
And he whistled, and shouted, and called them by name;
"Now, BLINKEN! now, RICE! now, BECERRA and TANDEN!
On, HAALAND! On AUSTIN! On, MCDONOUGH and YELLIN!
To the top of the portico! to inside the great hall!
Now dash away! dash away! dash away all!"
As dry leaves that before the wild hurricane fly,
To meet the Trump obstacle, they mount to the sky,
And up to the house-top the advisors they flew,
The corvette full of experience...Vice President Harris, too!
And then, ears tingling, Trump heard on the roof
The footsteps and treading...his plan? Not foolproof.
As he drew in his tiny hand, and was turning around,
Down the chimney Joe Biden came with a bound.
He was dressed all in blue, from his head to his foot,
And his clothes were untarnished, no ashes or soot;
A bundle of policies he had flung on his back,
And he looked presidential, for governing, a knack.
His eyes -- how they twinkled! his countenance merry!
A real happy warrior, with no time to tarry!
He came on a mission, ready to make his vow,
The Constitution needed righting, it was time, here and now;
The stump of a hammer he held tight underneath,
The nails to encircle with the implement’s teeth;
With fierce determination and resolute strength a-plenty,
He made for the mantel, now in the beast’s underbelly.
Past the unused maps and the dusty bookshelf,
Trump panicked when he saw him, in spite of himself;
A glare of his eye and a twist of his head,
It was then the Trumps knew, they had so much to dread;
He spoke not a word, but went straight to his work,
Removed ALL the stockings; then turned with a jerk,
And laying his finger aside of his nose,
Frowned at the big fraud, and up the chimney he rose;
He sprang to his ‘vette, to his team gave a whistle,
And away they all flew, the exhaust a dismissal.
The Trumps heard him exclaim, ere he drove out of sight,
SEE YOU TOMORROW AT THE INAUGURATION, DON’T YOU PUT UP A FIGHT!

www.TNDP.org [https://tndp.org]

All the best to you and yours in the New Year, Mary, Kris, Emily, Evan, Julio, Maria, and Sandra ‘Twas the Night Before the Inauguration (with apologies to Clement Clarke Moore) 'Twas the night before Inauguration, and all through the White House, All the creatures were stirring, even the orange-haired louse. The stockings were nailed to the chimney with fanfare, As if to say, "Try and take us down, try-hard, if you dare." Trump children were hiding, snug under rugs by their beds, While visions of staying forever danced in their heads; And Melania in her 'kerchief, and Trump, a MAGA hat, Had just barricaded in, still hoping for a coup d’etat, When out on the north lawn there arose such a clatter, They sprang from the bed to see what was the matter. Away to the window they flew like a flash, Tore open the shutters and threw up the sash. The moon on the crest of the new-fallen snow Gave the luster of daylight to the rose garden below, When, what to their plundering eyes should appear, But a Corvette Stingray, revving up in high gear, With a dashing bold driver, in blue head to toe, They knew in a moment it must be Uncle Joe. More rapid than eagles Joe’s cabinet came, And he whistled, and shouted, and called them by name; "Now, BLINKEN! now, RICE! now, BECERRA and TANDEN! On, HAALAND! On AUSTIN! On, MCDONOUGH and YELLIN! To the top of the portico! to inside the great hall! Now dash away! dash away! dash away all!" As dry leaves that before the wild hurricane fly, To meet the Trump obstacle, they mount to the sky, And up to the house-top the advisors they flew, The corvette full of experience...Vice President Harris, too! And then, ears tingling, Trump heard on the roof The footsteps and treading...his plan? Not foolproof. As he drew in his tiny hand, and was turning around, Down the chimney Joe Biden came with a bound. He was dressed all in blue, from his head to his foot, And his clothes were untarnished, no ashes or soot; A bundle of policies he had flung on his back, And he looked presidential, for governing, a knack. His eyes -- how they twinkled! his countenance merry! A real happy warrior, with no time to tarry! He came on a mission, ready to make his vow, The Constitution needed righting, it was time, here and now; The stump of a hammer he held tight underneath, The nails to encircle with the implement’s teeth; With fierce determination and resolute strength a-plenty, He made for the mantel, now in the beast’s underbelly. Past the unused maps and the dusty bookshelf, Trump panicked when he saw him, in spite of himself; A glare of his eye and a twist of his head, It was then the Trumps knew, they had so much to dread; He spoke not a word, but went straight to his work, Removed ALL the stockings; then turned with a jerk, And laying his finger aside of his nose, Frowned at the big fraud, and up the chimney he rose; He sprang to his ‘vette, to his team gave a whistle, And away they all flew, the exhaust a dismissal. The Trumps heard him exclaim, ere he drove out of sight, SEE YOU TOMORROW AT THE INAUGURATION, DON’T YOU PUT UP A FIGHT! www.TNDP.org [] PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR. GEETA MCMILLAN, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE. Tennessee Democratic Party 319 Plus Park Blvd Nashville, TN 37217 United States

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Delivering Relief and Looking Ahead
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501148702%2501100506%2b-%2b12-24-2020%2beNewsletter%250115647.22689450.4986369.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501148702%2501100506%2b-%2b12-24-2020%2beNewsletter%250115647.22689450.4986369.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **December 24, 2020** |
---|---|---|---
| | | 2020 has been a challenging year across our state and our country. We've lost friends and family members, and many have struggled to stay afloat financially. And just earlier this month, our state mourned the passing of a principled leader and tireless advocate for Maryland, former Senator Paul Sarbanes. His leadership in preserving the Chesapeake Bay, expanding access to affordable housing, and protecting Main Street investors and consumers from fraud and abusive financial practices are enduring testaments to his legacy of positive change. This is one more loss in a year that has been full of profound hardship, struggle, and pain for so many Marylanders - and has tested the will of every American. Reflecting back on this year, I'm heartened by the strength and resilience of our communities in weathering these challenging times together. I'm inspired by the frontline workers, researchers, and medical professionals who put themselves in harm's way to keep Americans safe in this time of crisis. And I'm grateful for all of those who have fought to protect our democracy, promote equality, and speak truth to power.
---
### Getting Coronavirus Aid Across the Finish Line
As I write this letter, we are waiting anxiously to determine whether President Trump is going to sign the new, desperately needed bipartisan coronavirus relief measure that Congress passed on Monday. The President has thrown a final curveball into the process by suggesting he may veto the bill. Doing so would further delay relief to American families and could trigger an avoidable government shutdown. I agree that the direct payments to Americans should be larger - and have supported previous attempts by Democrats to raise these payments to $1,200. But Republicans have repeatedly blocked these efforts, including a push just this morning by Democrats in the House of Representatives to raise the dollar amount of these stimulus checks to $2,000. Only Republicans are standing in the way of us getting this done. Now more than ever, Americans are feeling the weight of the pandemic. From watching more of their neighbors and family members fall ill, to struggling to pay mounting bills and put food on the table - the costs of this virus are all too real. That's why I was pleased Congress was able to come together this week to pass a bipartisan relief package that will bring more support to those who need it most. This package isn't perfect, but I'm glad that lawmakers on both sides of the aisle were able to find common ground and put this plan on the table, and I was pleased to have fought for many of the priorities that were included here and to have helped resolve the final dispute over the powers of the Federal Reserve. This bipartisan package makes important progress on provisions that will help Americans weather the storm this winter, including an extension of expanded unemployment benefits and the federal eviction moratorium, as well as rental and food assistance. It also includes direct payments to help millions of families and provides an infusion of funds to many of the programs that are key features of the bipartisan CARES Act, including a more-targeted Paycheck Protection Program focused on the small businesses most in need and those in hard-hit minority communities. In addition, the bill provides necessary resources for testing, tracing, and vaccine distribution. Throughout the negotiations, I worked with my colleagues to include a number of provisions that I've been fighting for since the beginning of the pandemic - including vital funding to extend economic relief for families and small businesses, support our transit systems, connect students, families and small businesses to broadband, and bolster SNAP and other nutrition assistance programs. That being said, I remain disappointed that while this package provides funding for schools, vaccine distribution, and other programs that will relieve budget pressures on state and local governments, it does not provide them with direct support. I strongly believe there is a need to ensure our cities and towns on the front lines of the fight against the pandemic have the resources necessary to keep our communities safe, provide emergency services, and keep first responders on the job. Also, while I'm pleased we got additional funds for broadband - including to support the costs for low-income families - I'm outraged that funding for students through the E-rate program for distance learning was made into a partisan issue by Senate Republicans. I will continue to fight on both of these fronts. This package does not include everything I had hoped for - and the Congress will need to work together with the Biden Administration to provide more support in the future. But this plan will help families, workers, and small businesses across the country battle the impact of this pandemic over the winter months, and it would have been unconscionable for Congress to leave without providing this essential help.
### Standing Up for Our Troops and National Defense
In addition to his veto threat on the COVID-19 relief package, the President yesterday vetoed the annual National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA). This vital legislation includes a pay raise for our troops, critical investments for Maryland's military installations, and support for key national security priorities. Trump's veto of this bill, which has passed Congress and been signed into law without fail every year for the last sixty years, is based on the worst of reasons. He's taking this unprecedented step because it does not include unrelated provisions to punish Twitter for fact-checking his lies and because it moves forward on renaming military bases that glorify those who fought with the Confederacy to protect slavery. It's clear that once again President Trump is putting his ego and his own self-interest before the interests of the American people, our armed forces, and our national security. This veto will be welcome news to our adversaries. I urge a Congressional override of this action as soon as possible.
### Supporting Maryland Priorities
I was glad to see Congress agree on a bipartisan government funding package to invest in crucial priorities across our country and prevent another shameful government shutdown. As a member of the Senate Appropriations Committee, I fought for [a number of key provisions](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-secures-key-maryland-funding-priorities-in-omnibus-package&redir_log=55695464116862) in the final spending bill that will directly benefit Marylanders - including funds to strengthen our schools, invest in health care services and biomedical research, support key federal facilities and installations in Maryland, modernize our infrastructure and transportation networks, and boost research and preservation efforts for the Chesapeake Bay. I was also pleased to see the Senate unanimously pass a number of bipartisan bills I've been working on throughout this Congress. First, the Senate passed my [Child Care Protection Improvement Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-burr-mcbath-applaud-senate-passage-of-bipartisan-child-care-protection-improvement-act&redir_log=928872845381057), legislation that I introduced alongside Senate and House colleagues that will bolster criminal background checks for childcare employees and better ensure our children are safe. It's now headed to the President's desk, and I urge him to sign it without delay. The Senate also passed my bipartisan [Protecting American Intellectual Property Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fsenate-passes-van-hollen-sasse-bill-to-punish-foreign-companies-individuals-for-ip-theft&redir_log=627573664903992), which mandates strong economic penalties on firms and individuals involved in stealing American intellectual property. The need could not be more urgent - especially after the series of cyberattacks we have witnessed aimed at the COVID-19 vaccine. The Senate also passed my [BUILD Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-fischer-bipartisan-build-act-passes-senate&redir_log=235237391664747), legislation to ensure nonprofits like Habitat for Humanity are not constrained by overly burdensome regulations, so they can get help where it's needed, fast. These bills must now pass the House of Representatives, and I hope that chamber will take action to move them to the President's desk. Finally, I'm pleased that my bipartisan [Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-sherman-gonzalez-applaud-holding-foreign-companies-accountable-acts-signing-into-law&redir_log=150132883141044) has been signed into law, so we can protect American investors and their retirement savings from foreign companies operating on U.S. stock exchanges. This action will safeguard the millions of families that rely on modest investments to retire, send their kids to college, and weather financial emergencies.
### Honoring the Memory of Henrietta Lacks with Action
The Senate has also passed the [Henrietta Lacks Enhancing Cancer Research Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fsenate-passes-van-hollen-led-legislation-to-honor-henrietta-lacks-improve-equity-in-cancer-research&redir_log=942808930471070), named after Marylander Henrietta Lacks who died of cervical cancer in 1951. While treating her for cancer, doctors took samples of her tumor and created the HeLa cell line from her remarkably resilient tissue - the first human cell line that was able to reproduce indefinitely. Without her or her family's knowledge, Henrietta Lacks had her cells used for medical research, which helped lead to some of medicine's most important breakthroughs, including the development of the polio vaccine and treatments for cancer, HIV/AIDS, leukemia, and Parkinson's disease. For too long, Henrietta Lacks' story went untold. While our nation will never be able to adequately express its gratitude for her historic contributions to medical research and the countless lives her cells have saved, this legislation is a step in the right direction. This Act would direct the Government Accountability Office to study and publish a report regarding barriers to participation in federally funded cancer clinical trials by populations that have been traditionally underrepresented in such trials. The bill will help ensure that all people - especially those from communities of color - are fairly represented in cancer trials and ultimately receive the treatments they deserve. I was honored to introduce this legislation alongside my friend, the late Elijah Cummings, and I'm proud that Henrietta Lacks' family supported the effort to improve cancer treatments in her name. This bill is now off to the President's desk, and I urge him to sign it into law immediately.
### Looking Toward the Year Ahead
Supreme Court Justice Thurgood Marshall once said, "The measure of a country's greatness is its ability to retain compassion in times of crisis." I'm hopeful that the inauguration of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris will usher in a new era of compassion in our politics and in our government's response to the ongoing public health and economic crisis. President-elect Biden has promised to be a President for all Americans - someone who will fight to better the lives of both those who voted for him and those who didn't. From day one, I'll be working with the Biden-Harris Administration to support America's workers, families, and small businesses as we continue to battle the COVID-19 pandemic. And I'll be fighting for Maryland priorities in our efforts to build back better: from rooting out systemic racism, to creating an economy and education system that provides all Americans with the opportunity to succeed, to passing a 21st century infrastructure bill that will expand access to broadband, strengthen public transit, and invest in green technologies. I look forward to working with the Biden-Harris Administration to address these pressing challenges facing our nation. Despite these difficult times, I hope you're able to find joy this holiday season with loved ones and family members, together or apart. This wasn't the year any of us expected, but I know that we will get through it. Our brightest days are still to come. I wish all Marylanders a happy and safe New Year. To 2021! Chris Van Hollen
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284 "Facebook")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241 "Twitter")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292 "Instagram")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326 "YouTube")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15647.22689450.4986369.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | December 24, 2020 | ---|---|---|--- | | | 2020 has been a challenging year across our state and our country. We've lost friends and family members, and many have struggled to stay afloat financially. And just earlier this month, our state mourned the passing of a principled leader and tireless advocate for Maryland, former Senator Paul Sarbanes. His leadership in preserving the Chesapeake Bay, expanding access to affordable housing, and protecting Main Street investors and consumers from fraud and abusive financial practices are enduring testaments to his legacy of positive change. This is one more loss in a year that has been full of profound hardship, struggle, and pain for so many Marylanders - and has tested the will of every American. Reflecting back on this year, I'm heartened by the strength and resilience of our communities in weathering these challenging times together. I'm inspired by the frontline workers, researchers, and medical professionals who put themselves in harm's way to keep Americans safe in this time of crisis. And I'm grateful for all of those who have fought to protect our democracy, promote equality, and speak truth to power. Getting Coronavirus Aid Across the Finish Line As I write this letter, we are waiting anxiously to determine whether President Trump is going to sign the new, desperately needed bipartisan coronavirus relief measure that Congress passed on Monday. The President has thrown a final curveball into the process by suggesting he may veto the bill. Doing so would further delay relief to American families and could trigger an avoidable government shutdown. I agree that the direct payments to Americans should be larger - and have supported previous attempts by Democrats to raise these payments to $1,200. But Republicans have repeatedly blocked these efforts, including a push just this morning by Democrats in the House of Representatives to raise the dollar amount of these stimulus checks to $2,000. Only Republicans are standing in the way of us getting this done. Now more than ever, Americans are feeling the weight of the pandemic. From watching more of their neighbors and family members fall ill, to struggling to pay mounting bills and put food on the table - the costs of this virus are all too real. That's why I was pleased Congress was able to come together this week to pass a bipartisan relief package that will bring more support to those who need it most. This package isn't perfect, but I'm glad that lawmakers on both sides of the aisle were able to find common ground and put this plan on the table, and I was pleased to have fought for many of the priorities that were included here and to have helped resolve the final dispute over the powers of the Federal Reserve. This bipartisan package makes important progress on provisions that will help Americans weather the storm this winter, including an extension of expanded unemployment benefits and the federal eviction moratorium, as well as rental and food assistance. It also includes direct payments to help millions of families and provides an infusion of funds to many of the programs that are key features of the bipartisan CARES Act, including a more-targeted Paycheck Protection Program focused on the small businesses most in need and those in hard-hit minority communities. In addition, the bill provides necessary resources for testing, tracing, and vaccine distribution. Throughout the negotiations, I worked with my colleagues to include a number of provisions that I've been fighting for since the beginning of the pandemic - including vital funding to extend economic relief for families and small businesses, support our transit systems, connect students, families and small businesses to broadband, and bolster SNAP and other nutrition assistance programs. That being said, I remain disappointed that while this package provides funding for schools, vaccine distribution, and other programs that will relieve budget pressures on state and local governments, it does not provide them with direct support. I strongly believe there is a need to ensure our cities and towns on the front lines of the fight against the pandemic have the resources necessary to keep our communities safe, provide emergency services, and keep first responders on the job. Also, while I'm pleased we got additional funds for broadband - including to support the costs for low-income families - I'm outraged that funding for students through the E-rate program for distance learning was made into a partisan issue by Senate Republicans. I will continue to fight on both of these fronts. This package does not include everything I had hoped for - and the Congress will need to work together with the Biden Administration to provide more support in the future. But this plan will help families, workers, and small businesses across the country battle the impact of this pandemic over the winter months, and it would have been unconscionable for Congress to leave without providing this essential help. Standing Up for Our Troops and National Defense In addition to his veto threat on the COVID-19 relief package, the President yesterday vetoed the annual National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA). This vital legislation includes a pay raise for our troops, critical investments for Maryland's military installations, and support for key national security priorities. Trump's veto of this bill, which has passed Congress and been signed into law without fail every year for the last sixty years, is based on the worst of reasons. He's taking this unprecedented step because it does not include unrelated provisions to punish Twitter for fact-checking his lies and because it moves forward on renaming military bases that glorify those who fought with the Confederacy to protect slavery. It's clear that once again President Trump is putting his ego and his own self-interest before the interests of the American people, our armed forces, and our national security. This veto will be welcome news to our adversaries. I urge a Congressional override of this action as soon as possible. Supporting Maryland Priorities I was glad to see Congress agree on a bipartisan government funding package to invest in crucial priorities across our country and prevent another shameful government shutdown. As a member of the Senate Appropriations Committee, I fought for in the final spending bill that will directly benefit Marylanders - including funds to strengthen our schools, invest in health care services and biomedical research, support key federal facilities and installations in Maryland, modernize our infrastructure and transportation networks, and boost research and preservation efforts for the Chesapeake Bay. I was also pleased to see the Senate unanimously pass a number of bipartisan bills I've been working on throughout this Congress. First, the Senate passed my , legislation that I introduced alongside Senate and House colleagues that will bolster criminal background checks for childcare employees and better ensure our children are safe. It's now headed to the President's desk, and I urge him to sign it without delay. The Senate also passed my bipartisan , which mandates strong economic penalties on firms and individuals involved in stealing American intellectual property. The need could not be more urgent - especially after the series of cyberattacks we have witnessed aimed at the COVID-19 vaccine. The Senate also passed my , legislation to ensure nonprofits like Habitat for Humanity are not constrained by overly burdensome regulations, so they can get help where it's needed, fast. These bills must now pass the House of Representatives, and I hope that chamber will take action to move them to the President's desk. Finally, I'm pleased that my bipartisan has been signed into law, so we can protect American investors and their retirement savings from foreign companies operating on U.S. stock exchanges. This action will safeguard the millions of families that rely on modest investments to retire, send their kids to college, and weather financial emergencies. Honoring the Memory of Henrietta Lacks with Action The Senate has also passed the , named after Marylander Henrietta Lacks who died of cervical cancer in 1951. While treating her for cancer, doctors took samples of her tumor and created the HeLa cell line from her remarkably resilient tissue - the first human cell line that was able to reproduce indefinitely. The bill will help ensure that all people - especially those from communities of color - are fairly represented in cancer trials and ultimately receive the treatments they deserve. I was honored to introduce this legislation alongside my friend, the late Elijah Cummings, and I'm proud that Henrietta Lacks' family supported the effort to improve cancer treatments in her name. Looking Toward the Year Ahead Supreme Court Justice Thurgood Marshall once said, "The measure of a country's greatness is its ability to retain compassion in times of crisis." I'm hopeful that the inauguration of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris will usher in a new era of compassion in our politics and in our government's response to the ongoing public health and economic crisis. President-elect Biden has promised to be a President for all Americans - someone who will fight to better the lives of both those who voted for him and those who didn't. From day one, I'll be working with the Biden-Harris Administration to support America's workers, families, and small businesses as we continue to battle the COVID-19 pandemic. And I'll be fighting for Maryland priorities in our efforts to build back better: from rooting out systemic racism, to creating an economy and education system that provides all Americans with the opportunity to succeed, to passing a 21st century infrastructure bill that will expand access to broadband, strengthen public transit, and invest in green technologies. I look forward to working with the Biden-Harris Administration to address these pressing challenges facing our nation. This wasn't the year any of us expected, but I know that we will get through it. Our brightest days are still to come. I wish all Marylanders a happy and safe New Year. To 2021! body:
This Christmas we wanted to get her something special... We need 1,000 grassroots supporters to 'Stand with Steel' by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT!
****

.

[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==)

Michelle has dedicated her life to advocating for the voiceless and providing resources to those who need them. She's opposed new taxes, stopped tax hikes, and even returned over $400 million to California taxpayers. And now she's taking this fight to the national level.

So this Christmas we wanted to get her something special... **We need 1,000 grassroots supporters to 'Stand with Steel' by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT!**
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==) | [](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==)****[STAND WITH STEEL](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==)****

We're giving Michelle a list of everyone who has joined Team Steel Christmas morning (_that 's tomorrow_) and we'd love for your name to be on it! [**We 're cutting it close, so sign the card by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT!**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==)

Michelle came to the United States as an immigrant looking for opportunity and a better life -- but instead found a broken political system that was taking advantage of those it was built to serve. She believes in California and she knows there's a better, brighter future ahead of us.

That's why, for Christmas, we want to show Michelle _we 're with her._ So please, if you believe in a better, brighter future for ALL Americans -- [**sign the card by midnight tonight!**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001dECI2WfMiQ5-hcXmoJx6UXAJDHnxYAWckVX1uzMQl7GvHX4KxhNVu54zEfCk0kykr6ybChkwA43cOqWV_Of9W7R0g3PtQlwLhLrpnp2Tb1PqsKHVcLW0gm94ckqrXZsdhnzfnyqofZT9lcO7OwtCavKqYVQFbelS4ZFfY5mIZnfZiQyu7wNSsGVAGr_6G3yryugzKEWOuwbUnB5KqdsmLOzRvTt7BzgXJKP5Ab7EFsq-KQfnejhm0Kc_K9EnwaqjMvy_SVbqO-tlao9ZPSPaQ9aKsS-j85Tyx2QDxQOZJTfthqcpGcOawOFJaYeNHcqF0glgmEgtD1r8kti1JylexVrpVsTzu89yWHGkNhTF_U5trCP1AFvjIn8L1Y5Xv_gl88X41ElAtei0PXlio249rw==&c=it9_d09zBw8ZWs_wgXx8HkbdnVlGH_hP8fPt0yoMu8-M9xwvXBFUMw==&ch=-YRpl06H8u-Kci9tfYKZmrLvHgXMvK0mh8K-W4gBPQmuMUVh10mgYg==)

Team Steel Holiday Elves

We need 1,000 grassroots supporters to 'Stand with Steel' by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT! ** . == Friend Michelle has dedicated her life to advocating for the voiceless and providing resources to those who need them. She's opposed new taxes, stopped tax hikes, and even returned over $400 million to California taxpayers. And now she's taking this fight to the national level. So this Christmas we wanted to get her something special... We need 1,000 grassroots supporters to 'Stand with Steel' by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT! == | ==** We're giving Michelle a list of everyone who has joined Team Steel Christmas morning (that 's tomorrow) and we'd love for your name to be on it! == Michelle came to the United States as an immigrant looking for opportunity and a better life -- but instead found a broken political system that was taking advantage of those it was built to serve. She believes in California and she knows there's a better, brighter future ahead of us. That's why, for Christmas, we want to show Michelle we 're with her. So please, if you believe in a better, brighter future for ALL Americans -- == Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays, Team Steel Holiday Elves == Paid for by Michelle Steel for Congress P.O Box 472 Surfside, CA 90743== == == == == | | Michelle Steel for Congress | 8907 Warner Ave Suite 220, Huntington Beach, CA 92647 | | Sent by mailto:action@standupforliberty.net powered by

subject: Happy Holidays
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends for John Carney |
body:
Friend,

As we turn to the new year, we honor service and sacrifice. We remember and celebrate loved ones, and we renew our faith in what we can overcome together. We join you in shared determination to meet the promise of the season.

And we wish your family strength and peace.
John, Tracey, Sam and Jimmy Carney

body:
All the Democrats want for Christmas is to:
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ELx1SjsZkLjWLOOdAOB6rdGiXf3JNlWKnP_FWFbJ_UlLrOISDVV-lSwaagIFIjomyORbvJhzSH3huEI1wcOjpYb8pHx-t-ZHfxQk3WlxuhD0bsblm826gtlxQSircL77RjcNLTmuuX7NDW78v3xMlE5e8mwJghYxygcWrU-dyyELWfBwKckb59fuyTK7uabI2dSH6KKx1O2xi2f8_lm7_nzx71FgPqWZZaPjL5mq_iKz0DXSVT0-yIuBtEDPmxMAfkwDCS61T2HKA7pO9a-jLgkvKHIcLFAYdDwkGwrx2pV2xMrrI3ANPD3Z2UuxoSh9AHIEDgc00MzpSDEIYUnnmzFvjB-7wADN17IMep9W6EPHA4ioGhFUOX3e17lOjqobQ9FibKLZM4FlXEHTzoZ0Y-lxWQ7c8SaHyY6sVsAwiRXlgSkZnxkX0ao_zdRzggZT&c=yE19gwQjYTPwjapXTZnPh_6YTug3ng1zx44fX5w2pSdFmPGJW98qIg==&ch=tbEO2lBXaxoqPOxv6P0j788KNCX5H0oOOVkTcWKyzzkLw1Ds8TQzNA==)

Friend -

All the Democrats want for Christmas is to:

❌ Take control of the Senate
❌ Raise taxes on hard working Americans
❌ Cut our military funding
❌ Defund the police
❌ Force one-size-fits all lockdowns
❌Eliminate private health insurance
❌ Confiscate your guns
❌ Pack the Courts
❌ Implement taxpayer-funded abortion on demand
❌Open borders

That is why we're calling on you Friend. **David Perdue is fighting for a safer, more prosperous Georgia.** America needs David in Washington to fight for our values and save our Senate Majority. We need your help to ensure that we block the Democrat wishlist and continue on the prosperous path that President Trump has paved for us.
**
_Can David count on you to get in the fight? _**

name: Kelly Loeffler
subject: How the Democrats stole Christmas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Georgians for Kelly Loeffler
body:
Every God-fearing Patriot loved Christmas a lot. But the Democrats who governed them surely did not. They hated the holidays and all of their cheer. So they used a pandemic to elevate fear.
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Lz3fMtiKeIxqOUOTbjfF4h9Cnj2TYlU_55F7e5WJNL5-n7mKIWxrMOkEJdFkGgMYVhlEoS8kMyZq5ogGLERmYbcmr-Z8pH9FOYrAxhP2vXlQyR4OcI8YhkTG6UN9cmQ6BfFoCOsCFoHnGc8YOE3B3JClbt_OXWi9TWMjLBd7lwAHTnDk64dIKUdAQNBkOelqiImi009PdIcvP1vuNniESP0S8GS57noijlcez7FD9QLgq7sZodkvY_mGF6zUlfS_wrL8PaA_iy1rhEXDDPo1qYbgOJFzSCIHO2fhwB_d0vbkhZsJZkfarf8R7QN9m3VvuGCyrQxZRkQ1jC5GgKMOTEh73H5-cgg8whrpLeMlcoXe2rGzwMuYc-zJ-hOy23wvRy9sDglOsU30nE70gJYKSHiHtq9C-WygNOPR8oaM60tvgQrHr3PL1yEIDMzOx97DLF2Ybk4ox85BvfqeJYodgw==&c=lKFcMknbSLpQiGdN9kgccP5FmdItQe5v3VnkDv1cDRVIids48OaQmg==&ch=9PMJc0P-Se3ihWrRwA9lnpNRLlU8GzxiFyadiQ4bfYqetjqfbM2nhw==)
**_Every God-fearing Patriot loved Christmas a lot.
But the Democrats who governed them surely did not. _**
**_They hated the holidays and all of their cheer.
So they used a pandemic to elevate fear. _**
**_It could be, perhaps, that they were power hungry.
Or maybe, just maybe, they were in it for money. _**
**_But I think the most likely reason of all
Is that they want our economy two sizes too small. _**
**_The Democrats dislike that we still have our freedom.
Which is why, in the Senate, we really must beat 'em. _**
**Friend, the liberal Democrats who want to cancel Christmas over COVID-19 are the same ones who are trying to beat me in my Senate runoff in Georgia.**

The Democrats will shut down and ruin this country if they are given more control. I need Patriots like you to stand with me and fight back.

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
The holiday season -- a celebration of light, love, and family amidst the shortest and coldest days of the year -- is more important now than ever before. After a challenging year, we're winding down into a winter that seems a little colder and a little less familiar. It would be easy to think that with our normal traditions disrupted, the holiday spirit is gone.
But Rhode Islanders are resilient. We carry the holiday spirit with us in every phone call or Zoom chat, in every mailed package, and in every light display. We've found ways to connect with each other and share in this time of joy and harmony despite the circumstances that may physically separate us.
And while we celebrate the unbreakable ties of love that connect us across distance, let's keep in our thoughts those who have lost a loved one this year and our Gold Star families. Please, reach out to the people in your life and let them know that you care.
No matter what or how you celebrate, let's all come together and lend each other the warmth and light that we need to make it through these dark times. Brighter days lie ahead in 2021. Happy holidays, and God bless Rhode Island.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19. clean_body: | | | | | | | Hello friend, The holiday season -- a celebration of light, love, and family amidst the shortest and coldest days of the year -- is more important now than ever before. After a challenging year, we're winding down into a winter that seems a little colder and a little less familiar. It would be easy to think that with our normal traditions disrupted, the holiday spirit is gone. But Rhode Islanders are resilient. We carry the holiday spirit with us in every phone call or Zoom chat, in every mailed package, and in every light display. We've found ways to connect with each other and share in this time of joy and harmony despite the circumstances that may physically separate us. And while we celebrate the unbreakable ties of love that connect us across distance, let's keep in our thoughts those who have lost a loved one this year and our Gold Star families. Please, reach out to the people in your life and let them know that you care. No matter what or how you celebrate, let's all come together and lend each other the warmth and light that we need to make it through these dark times. Brighter days lie ahead in 2021. Happy holidays, and God bless Rhode Island. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19. If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use ==. | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov powered by

| _**Merry (Early) Christmas!**_ As we count down the remaining hours until Christmas, I'm looking forward to celebrating some of our family's most cherished traditions at home. We'll share in the blessings we've been granted, and I hope you and your family can as well.

Today I also wanted to share with you an infographic we've put together detailing your Indiana Republican Party's successes at the ballot box in 2020. It's a report card, of sorts. Together, we worked hard this year -- 2.65 million phone calls, 839,000 door knocks, and 2 million text messages. And, as Hoosiers know, hard work leads to results.

Thank you all for that hard work in 2020. Merry Christmas!

\- Kyle Hupfer

name: Heidi Sloan
subject: Join the Effort to Help to Empower & Support Frontline Workers
body:
Frontline workers bear the brunt of this pandemic. Join me in supporting an organization working to empower and organize these workers. | Hi Friend, We all know that our country is not responding well to this pandemic. From a failure to pass a meaningful stimulus package to haphazard mask enforcement, it is clear that we have failed to manage this public health crisis. But what may be less obvious is that the brunt of this confusion and mismanagement falls on frontline workers. They are the ones who can't work from home. They are the ones who must ask belligerent customers to wear masks. They are the ones risking their health everyday. We rely on these workers. And yet, they often labor in terrible, unsafe conditions. That is why the Emergency Workplace Organizing Committee was founded this past March. EWOC is dedicated to helping nonunion, frontline workers connect with the support and resources they need to organize their workplaces and demand better conditions. Already, EWOC has connected with over 1,900 workers and launched dozens of labor organizing campaigns.
And they aren't scaling down their work any time soon -- in fact, they are getting ready to expand. In 2021 they are going to increase their outreach and training programs. So if you or anyone you know are ready to organize and demand a better, safer workplace, fill out their [Talk with an Organizer form](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDNkTxTwePKlqncwZMz1uJoprKJLEZ9BSrQ1PHc0NGNJgml6rvsQGcqIAzVUEoOfoNTm14uFXO-axBFZqiW9njqYzzugNbg0XOZc2latay0wzRKm8MDdlRWmjAGmAyvpuyJdyck1RA1X4dNtxxaqDUGuY-YNpI8ro-s5iHpHTiVgwgZiY9skmjMwgATzl1mzCdH2cOb8RYCoPdh6yA5XBfOTxu1B7MzaiuQ7GdQRI27nk6PdFBq-JSnGmRR46k8UGMALwjMkLSwlAlo6WmcsOCENWGRy-XDa7eDlqqkdJShxta_bzcdG7bzRXzOdHmWbKXIda7bz44SJXIdOp6IqCYqINvzU8C5ZZZOvKRc3A2ykv/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h0/ALUz7PKOkdfza_7_0zkwwZZgadSW7fIjt1lTsGmiEdA). This is one of the best ways to get the support and help you need to organize a nonunion workplace.
---
[ Talk to a Labor Organizer ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDNkTxTwePKlqncwZMz1uJoprKJLEZ9BSrQ1PHc0NGNJgml6rvsQGcqIAzVUEoOfoNXgPs2azmTCvcgm_vwYe06Fx01PJ8YzismkbVHitV-x7tzVLQ4JZivNZOqZITuQSc4LYkVp64qBZlwWLP1B_r5O-dsmhDBPx9SErdmsDHLA1QUwV80AC8A3GVfJPTeWjzdAnQBp9bUs8PyBR2tUvqO2OjY6vq6Bobz0TcRW3S9oNm9njBvzWxWOBREKiTLLkvNmfclsWH20Cw4zFNRuLmnRaraUyLwHBGKOoDAZx6j1_AK3zHoS_QBrvqD2G-5KLSHAI_uO3IgOS3rS9d7juT9Ryztyv5dLEwDzGx402N0Y5/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h1/cBXJxDSWm2M22aFI8XpITNXKG8FVUdLOH_frbmzEBnA)
---
If you don't need to organize your own workplace, consider volunteering with EWOC! There are lots of ways to support the organization, from comms to outreach to labor support teams. [You can sign up to join the EWOC team here](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDNkTxTwePKlqncwZMz1uJory8pdn6-GQAEv6gidDBu7QTZWG-00AWdizodq81xGHI1dOre8h2AciCM1QMmD_h4dRY1XVyB3lDIwf7VXia7eTOX8vJ4cONs6zGx87-_aLuajs719ULpajk1P8I3RFMvHlDepE6JxK7IqstHxxMJvgqgJZWqS34fUzKdfYDnCtvx9Q7440_ukY2fqPjuF53JTK69fzobnUoOkO5nCtYosyS9N8WQizEf7usVdLQfAdC2paN-gViAjNEsNAVDBmbL70sqd7N2tfWuLqZ7td-id5OjUZa4rZs6CUY9c50_qPqyNzkaF72AESmIIqi6XCOzjZ5yZXeV-gwg9UUk_sQbnh/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h2/PLao506LGw5FE0bImuWDuI8YGdulmUf4KfYNYd33vC0)[.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDNkTxTwePKlqncwZMz1uJory8pdn6-GQAEv6gidDBu7QSc3dKh0eMwvGk1yM6yOrlDcwHOlFf8I6410WiLMqITNVIxO1IQ6iq69LUipeGSvfC5tYoNXMl7QXSkCIZE-hmB8C1Ma7A5-0LJxnx3cdz9J8QZsv9q92xA1_ZWR4imtt7yGC88_aTPHr6BiG264pvHp3Sy5nCyB6osy9dqgNc3Nfy8TcDl9M_7-WMEGf5aDzf9b1JqDk6miZwDNOT2lBJN2dSI9vw14Sd8ulIhEGKR675g6jYgZh1lI85Mpv1pCLTyiFD9QDixyP0_CA_BVxf1c3Uui6oET1tQJkRm5123GZcY6oDJVZbvdpp5sW0ghX/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h3/4Idoyox1cy3zU5djs1DsV9f-V4oFFtdGG6AsMg-Z880) Finally, EWOC is working to raise $15,000 by the end of the year. This money will go towards continuing and expanding their effort to connect frontline workers with the resources and support they need to organize. [If you are able, please consider making a contribution today.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xJPaWcKb4NYEGHEw5maOm2ANINJo77PcVvO-x-ApTQKmN_rxL8kKETF16sD4RKLL1K-Y1qHy2s2_71pUDMaOdssxmAyBnELYz2lFCWC6RjhALulCSCqQRog2aMCCA_w0k4FfFP9pqtD0ZN1_H9lLvjcXNnfRmFeRbBYheqzQ1bFqsd8_--0MgxGecrTrRpaysZlrf2KJXIVR6jzUp1n6Gxh5YrDZk0CuO6RcJcW6Fva422evxlB23wVLBOKke7WM6EK0wJe089cEP2tSf-cT2irYJ8r5MRUlyqivavOSCMzKFPKtxUblJYlC9Vyy_M8NA51V85JGNBAqCTWvpgZa3-53L2s6Jy4lLkd3UOa0PuvI1GOPMdhK8JhETrKsFlV3UUI8nCzLH4zkbvt1ehyWbV/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h4/edj8j1Ymfhtt8W4f1Ii_YGjbjNH5kQC883oVJKVVqrQ)
---
[ Make a Contribution ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xJPaWcKb4NYEGHEw5maOm2ANINJo77PcVvO-x-ApTQKmAepChybafNR-48Snnm4rN6_YT6dsyZE6gIomqPglWQCU67zqvLckwO8Plw6lvkFWZt7oIpCcTGwB7POiF_2YnAzPBM3Rn0HxdY-eml18dS5VX0HGrQfAKVt9h2rKoVQBVaJPD6P9jUBRYnmV5V3rnoc90tt34xcvwYKWp4vl7SuF-tAnVaEpxjqLpW8FhBhd946xF7Lck_i7DK4Ivbx6NpsXsC8WreAobEh68hgh90RCZ1lT1aRz9PBqqrKTIZei7y9eo-JTjwHNQ6Mw9mRqt3e-BSV5JIe75Dc6K4fV0nta-F6XznoX8o47ZEDmpBRdUcItz2vYYvsCzZb5l3TAZFIwTPLxTKMb8Ggsi4Q53x/37z/kx2b3NlkSVOIJsy3JpWtcw/h5/Hxnr3LCel6KDbcqkAjPxrWOUbro8DUZANJbewPLG8qw)
---
Thank you in advance for your generosity and for supporting this organization. I know you believe a better world is possible and are in this struggle for the long-haul. Empowering workers is critical to building the world we want.
In solidarity, Heidi

name: Patrick Murphy
subject: Happy Holidays from our family to yours!
body:
Peter,

We wish you and your family a very Merry Christmas and Happy Holiday! No doubt this 2020 holiday season is like no other, but things will be better in 2021 and we all still have much to be grateful for.

Like many American families, we unfortunately lost a cousin to COVID-19 a few weeks ago. In spite of all the challenges, our family has had a great year and been blessed in many ways. My daughter Maggie turned 14, is in 8th grade, and is 5’7. My son Jack turned 11, is in 5th grade, and wishes he was 5’7.
We hope you and your families are all safe, and will continue to keep you and your family in our nightly prayers, as well as those who have been sick or struggling, and the over 170,000 troops spending this holiday season overseas on our behalf, fighting to keep out nation safe.

Looking forward to seeing you ‘in person’ at some point in 2021.

Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays!

Patrick

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please https://act.myngp.com/el/ulyxCZdsLVwu9Qr1UOEgrGJHqvmzQQ4OabHNdsMzE3w=/M7ZkXekyzG2hlWfrTIadukLmToOy-FX--kDCK2yUnlY=.

| Friends,

As this year comes to a close, I wanted to thank you for your unwavering support. It has been my highest honor to serve Georgians and represent each of you in Washington this year.

I hope your holiday season is filled with joy, celebration, and cherished time with friends and loved ones.

From our family to yours, Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays!

\- Senator David Perdue

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: WATCH: Merry Christmas from President Trump and the First Lady
body:
President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump wish all Americans a very Merry Christmas! | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1e5e2c5494&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • December 24, 2020
---
| | |
# **WATCH: Merry Christmas from President Trump and the First Lady**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=08777991c8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump** wish all Americans a Merry Christmas and a wonderful holiday season. Despite the challenges posed by this year's global pandemic, the American people continue to display incredible courage and patriotism.

**The bond that unites us** as citizens is stronger than any adversity we face. As of this month, doses from _two_ new Coronavirus vaccines are now being administered to Americans across the country. This remarkable achievement is a testament to the strength and ingenuity that defines the American spirit.

**"We are grateful** for all of the scientists, researchers, manufacturing workers, and service members who have worked tirelessly to make this breakthrough possible," President Trump said. "It is truly a Christmas miracle."

**The President and First Lady thank** our first responders, law enforcement officers, doctors, nurses, and many others who made sacrifices this year to keep Americans safe. “Through this great challenge, we have been inspired by the kindness and courage of citizens across this country,” the First Lady said.

**_On behalf of the entire First Family, Merry Christmas and Happy New Year!_**
---
|
---
|
